validated_gaps.title: Execution Sandboxing for AI Agents

demand_signals.signal: Multiple Show HN projects building runtime guardrails and kill switches for AI agents
All shipped sandbox features by early 2026, indicating massive demand
Published practical security guidance for sandboxing agentic workflows
WebAssembly could solve AI agents' most dangerous security gap - containers insufficient
GKE Agent Sandbox launched as dedicated product for AI agent isolation
Standard Docker/runc isolation no longer sufficient for untrusted AI agent code

validated_gaps.competition_notes: Cloud providers (Cloudflare, GKE) offering basic sandbox, but no unified cross-platform agent sandboxing tool exists. MicroVM and Wasm approaches fragmented.

validated_gaps.title: MCP Protocol Security Gateway

demand_signals.signal: Published dedicated MCP Top 10 vulnerabilities list in 2026
MCP Servers identified as 'The New Shadow IT for AI in 2026'
Published 'The MCP AuthN/Z Nightmare' detailing fundamental protocol security flaws
Published 'The Agentic Trust Deficit: MCP's Authentication Vacuum'
MCP's 2026 roadmap makes enterprise readiness a top priority (WorkOS analysis)
Developer built 7 MCP servers for security tools, finding the protocol was the easy part but security was the hard part
Multiple 'Best MCP Gateways 2026' comparison articles indicate market forming

validated_gaps.competition_notes: Lasso Security offers open-source MCP gateway. Obot and ContextForge also emerging. Thales planning MCP security gateway. Market is nascent and fragmented.

validated_gaps.title: AI Model Supply Chain Security

demand_signals.signal: 36% of ClawHub skills contain detectable prompt injection, 13% contain critical security issues
AI-service leaks surged 81% with 29M secrets hitting public GitHub in 2026
Protect AI acquired by Palo Alto Networks; Cisco bought Robust Intelligence for $400M - indicating acquirer demand

validated_gaps.competition_notes: Protect AI Guardian (Palo Alto) and Cisco AI Defense are the only serious players. Both are enterprise-only. No open-source or mid-market solution.

validated_gaps.title: Agent Identity Management for SMBs/Startups

demand_signals.source: Gravitee State of AI Agent Security 2026
demand_signals.signal: 45.6% of teams rely on shared API keys for agent authentication; only 21.9% treat agents as identity-bearing entities
68% of organizations cannot distinguish human from AI agent activity
Oasis Security raised $120M specifically for AI agent identity security
AI Agents described as 'The Next Wave Identity Dark Matter - Powerful, Invisible, and Unmanaged'

validated_gaps.competition_notes: Okta, Microsoft, BeyondTrust all launching but exclusively enterprise. No open-source or SMB-accessible agent identity solution exists.

validated_gaps.title: Multi-Agent Security Orchestration

demand_signals.signal: Only 24.4% of organizations have full visibility into agent-to-agent communication
Named securing AI agents 'the defining cybersecurity challenge of 2026'
75% of leaders won't let security concerns slow their AI agent deployment, creating forced demand for runtime multi-agent security

validated_gaps.competition_notes: No dedicated multi-agent security orchestration tool. Cisco AI Defense and Microsoft Agent 365 partially address it but as part of larger platforms.

validated_gaps.title: Shadow AI Discovery and Governance for Midmarket

demand_signals.signal: Threads expressing frustration over shadow AI tools adopted bottom-up evading oversight
86% of organizations report no visibility into AI data flows; 1,200 unofficial AI apps per enterprise

validated_gaps.competition_notes: WitnessAI, Lasso, Zenity all offer shadow AI detection but at enterprise pricing. Reco.ai also competes. Midmarket gap remains.

validated_gaps.title: Unified Developer-First Security SDK

demand_signals.signal: 6+ Show HN projects in 2025-2026 building DIY guardrails, firewalls, and security layers - indicating developers want but can't find unified solutions
Three structural problems with AI agents: siloed memory, setup complexity, and cost opacity that nobody is solving
Proliferation of small, single-purpose security tools (TSZ, Guardrail Layer, various firewalls) shows fragmentation developers must navigate
Only 13 companies focus specifically on AI/LLM security despite $414M in funding - indicating room for new entrants

validated_gaps.competition_notes: Arcjet is closest but limited to prompt injection. No comprehensive developer-first SDK that combines guardrails + identity + observability + compliance.

validated_gaps.title: AI Agent Incident Response and Kill Switch

demand_signals.signal: Most organizations can monitor agents but cannot stop them when something goes wrong - governance-containment gap
Developer built LLM firewall specifically with 'kill switch for production incidents'
88% of organizations reported AI agent security incidents in past year, 92.7% in healthcare

validated_gaps.competition_notes: No dedicated tool. Some platforms (Zenity, Microsoft) include basic response capabilities but no standardized incident response framework for AI agents.

validated_gaps.title: Cost Transparency and Predictable Pricing

demand_signals.signal: Cost opacity identified as one of three structural problems nobody is solving
Discussions about C-suite deploying expensive tools without adequate staffing

validated_gaps.competition_notes: Only Cisco AI Defense Explorer offers free tier. Arcjet has freemium model. Most tools require 'contact sales'.

validated_gaps.title: Pre-Deployment Security Validation Pipeline

demand_signals.signal: AI coding agents keep repeating decade-old security mistakes, produce working software but with gaps in auth, rate limiting
As coders adopt AI agents, security pitfalls lurk - no shift-left solution for agent deployment

validated_gaps.competition_notes: Promptfoo offers CI/CD integration for testing but not as a deployment gate. No dedicated CI/CD security gate for AI agent deployment.
